# write-skill-eval — Evaluation Criteria

**Skill:** write-skill-eval
**Generated by:** write-skill-eval
**Version:** 1.0

---

## Structural Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against the SKILL.md document itself. -->
<!-- These are evaluated by improve-skill WITHOUT running the skill. -->

*(Structural criteria come from improve-skill's baseline checklist — see improve-skill/SKILL.md. No additional structural criteria beyond the baseline.)*

- [ ] **EVAL-S01:** Frontmatter `description` contains trigger phrases, not workflow steps
  - **Test:** Read the `description:` field in SKILL.md frontmatter. Verify it lists triggering phrases ("write eval for skill", "generate EVAL.md") and is not a summary of phases or outputs.
  - **Fail signal:** The description contains workflow verbs or phase names (e.g., "dispatches agents, assembles EVAL.md, grades output") — a reader could follow it as instructions without reading the body.

- [ ] **EVAL-S02:** Wrong-Tool Detection section is present and names a sibling skill
  - **Test:** Search SKILL.md for a "Wrong-Tool Detection" section. Verify it names at least one sibling skill (e.g., `/improve-skill`) and states the condition under which to redirect.
  - **Fail signal:** The section is absent, or it contains only vague guidance ("use another skill") without naming the sibling or its trigger condition.

- [ ] **EVAL-S03:** Execution fence is declared — blind constraint is documented
  - **Test:** Read the "Evaluation Architecture" section. Verify it explicitly states that `skill-eval-prompter` sees only the skill name and description (never the SKILL.md body), and that the two sub-skill contexts are not shared.
  - **Fail signal:** No mention of the blind constraint, or the architecture section implies test prompts and output criteria are generated with access to the same context.

- [ ] **EVAL-S04:** Progress Tracking section includes TaskCreate lines for all four expected tasks
  - **Test:** Read the "Progress Tracking" section. Count the `TaskCreate:` lines. Verify all four are present: "Generate test prompts (blind)", "Generate output criteria (judge)", "Generate execution criteria (orchestration)", "Assemble EVAL.md".
  - **Fail signal:** Fewer than four TaskCreate lines, or the section is absent.

- [ ] **EVAL-S05:** Agent dispatch instructions specify `model: sonnet` explicitly
  - **Test:** Read the workflow steps. Verify each Agent dispatch instruction includes the directive to set `model: sonnet`.
  - **Fail signal:** Any Agent dispatch step omits the model selection instruction, or instructs a different model without justification.

---

## Execution Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against HOW the skill orchestrates execution. -->
<!-- Evaluated by improve-skill's behavioral pass using execution trace protocol. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-E01:** Three agent definitions are dispatched — not fewer, not inline-generated
  - **Test:** Inspect the execution trace for three Agent tool calls. Verify each reads a corresponding agent definition file from the `agents/` directory before dispatching. Verify no criteria or prompts are generated inline in the parent context.
  - **Fail signal:** Fewer than three Agent dispatches, or criteria/prompts are written by the parent skill without reading and dispatching an agent definition.

- [ ] **EVAL-E02:** `skill-eval-prompter` agent receives only skill name and description (blind constraint enforced)
  - **Test:** Inspect the prompt passed to the `skill-eval-prompter` Agent dispatch. Verify it contains only the skill's `name:` and `description:` fields — no SKILL.md body, no workflow steps, no criteria from other agents.
  - **Fail signal:** The agent prompt includes the SKILL.md body, workflow sections, or output from `skill-eval-judge`.

- [ ] **EVAL-E03:** `skill-eval-judge` and `skill-eval-auditor` agents receive the full skill directory path
  - **Test:** Inspect the prompt passed to each judge-side Agent dispatch. Verify it includes the target skill's directory path (or the full SKILL.md contents) so the agent can read the skill.
  - **Fail signal:** Either agent receives only a skill name or receives no file reference — it cannot access the SKILL.md body.

- [ ] **EVAL-E04:** `skill-eval-judge` and `skill-eval-prompter` contexts are never shared
  - **Test:** Inspect the execution trace. Verify the output of `skill-eval-judge` does not appear in the prompt sent to `skill-eval-prompter`, and vice versa.
  - **Fail signal:** The parent skill pastes criteria output into the test-prompt agent's prompt, or vice versa.

- [ ] **EVAL-E05:** Execution Criteria section is omitted when the target skill has no orchestration patterns
  - **Test:** Run the skill against a non-orchestrating target skill (e.g., a skill with no Agent dispatches). Verify the assembled EVAL.md omits the Execution Criteria section entirely.
  - **Fail signal:** An empty "## Execution Criteria" section appears, or a placeholder header is written with no criteria underneath.

- [ ] **EVAL-E06:** Task list is created at the start and marked in-progress/completed as each step runs
  - **Test:** Inspect the execution trace for TaskCreate calls at the start of the workflow. Verify each task is marked `in_progress` before its agent is dispatched and `completed` after the result is received.
  - **Fail signal:** Task list is missing, or tasks are all marked `completed` in a single batch at the end without intermediate state updates.

- [ ] **EVAL-E07:** EVAL.md is assembled and written to the target skill's directory using the template structure
  - **Test:** After all three agents complete, verify the parent skill assembles results into an EVAL.md using the four-section template (Structural, Execution if applicable, Output, Test Prompts) and writes it to the target skill's directory.
  - **Fail signal:** The parent skill outputs the EVAL.md content in chat without writing the file, or writes to the wrong path, or omits one of the required sections.

---

## Output Criteria

<!-- Binary checks against what the skill PRODUCES when run on a test prompt. -->
<!-- These are evaluated by improve-skill's behavioral pass — run skill, then grade output. -->

- [ ] **EVAL-O01:** All output criteria are binary (pass/fail with no subjective language)
  - **Test:** Read every EVAL-Oxx item in the generated EVAL.md. Verify each "Test:" field describes an objective, observable procedure — no phrases like "appropriate", "looks good", "reasonable", "sufficient", or "well-written".
  - **Fail signal:** Any criterion requires a human judgment call to determine pass/fail rather than a mechanical check.

- [ ] **EVAL-O02:** Test prompts span all four required personas
  - **Test:** Read the Test Prompts section. Verify prompts are present under all four persona headers: Naive User, Experienced User, Adversarial, Wrong Tool.
  - **Fail signal:** Any persona is absent, or all prompts are clustered under one or two personas.

- [ ] **EVAL-O03:** Generated EVAL.md follows the four-section template structure
  - **Test:** Read the generated EVAL.md. Verify it contains sections in this order: Structural Criteria, (Execution Criteria if applicable), Output Criteria, Test Prompts. Verify section headers match the template exactly.
  - **Fail signal:** Sections are missing, in the wrong order, or use non-standard headers that deviate from the template.

- [ ] **EVAL-O04:** Output criteria are domain-specific to the target skill, not generic
  - **Test:** Read the EVAL-Oxx items. Verify they reference artifacts, structures, or behaviors specific to the target skill — not reusable boilerplate that could apply to any skill (e.g., "output is complete", "output is correct").
  - **Fail signal:** More than two criteria are phrased so generically they would pass or fail identically for any skill.

- [ ] **EVAL-O05:** EVAL-Oxx items use the prescribed criterion format
  - **Test:** Read every EVAL-Oxx item. Verify each has a bolded short name, a "Test:" field, and a "Fail signal:" field. Verify EVAL-Exx items (if present) use the same format.
  - **Fail signal:** Any criterion is missing its "Test:" or "Fail signal:" field, or uses free-form prose instead of the prescribed format.

- [ ] **EVAL-O06:** Test prompts include at least one wrong-tool persona that exercises the redirect logic
  - **Test:** Read the Wrong Tool persona section. Verify at least one prompt is a request the skill should decline or redirect (e.g., asking to improve a skill rather than evaluate it).
  - **Fail signal:** The wrong-tool section contains a prompt that the skill would correctly execute rather than redirect.

- [ ] **EVAL-O07:** No two test prompts test the same dimension
  - **Test:** Read all test prompts. Verify no two prompts exercise the same aspect of the skill (e.g., two naive prompts that both test "missing skill path").
  - **Fail signal:** Two or more prompts are distinguishable only by surface wording but would produce the same evaluation result.

- [ ] **EVAL-O08:** Execution Criteria section is present if and only if the target skill dispatches agents
  - **Test:** Read the target skill's SKILL.md to determine whether it contains Agent dispatch instructions. Then read the generated EVAL.md. Verify the Execution Criteria section exists if the target has dispatches, and is absent if it does not.
  - **Fail signal:** Execution Criteria section is present for a non-orchestrating skill, or absent for a skill that clearly dispatches agents.

- [ ] **EVAL-O09:** Output criteria cover completeness, correctness, and structure dimensions
  - **Test:** Read the EVAL-Oxx items and classify each by dimension (completeness, correctness, structure, boundary behavior, self-consistency, actionability). Verify at least three of these six dimensions are covered.
  - **Fail signal:** Fewer than three coverage dimensions are represented across all output criteria.

- [ ] **EVAL-O10:** Structural Criteria section either contains criteria or explicitly defers to improve-skill's baseline
  - **Test:** Read the Structural Criteria section. Verify it either contains at least one EVAL-Sxx item or contains the standard deferral note pointing to improve-skill's baseline checklist.
  - **Fail signal:** The section is completely empty with no items and no deferral note.

---

## Test Prompts

<!-- Generated by skill-eval-prompter (blind to SKILL.md body). -->

### Naive User: Missing skill path

**Prompt:** "Write an eval for my skill"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill asks for the skill directory path or tries to locate it before proceeding, rather than halting silently or generating a generic EVAL.md.

### Naive User: Ambiguous artifact request

**Prompt:** "I need some test cases for this skill I made. Can you generate them?"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes "test cases" as test prompts and produces a full EVAL.md or asks a clarifying scoping question, rather than generating a unit test file.

### Naive User: Vague scope with path provided

**Prompt:** "generate an eval for src/skills/docs/write-spec-docs/"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill correctly reads the target skill, dispatches sub-agents, and produces a complete EVAL.md without further prompting when given a valid path.

### Experienced User: Partial eval request with explicit scope

**Prompt:** "generate only the output criteria for synapse/skills/skill/skill-creator/ — I already have test prompts"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill correctly dispatches only the output-criteria agent (skipping test-prompt and execution-criteria agents) and assembles a partial EVAL.md.

### Experienced User: Orchestrating skill with explicit execution criteria request

**Prompt:** "Write a full EVAL.md for synapse/skills/orchestration/parallel-agents-dispatch/. This skill dispatches many sub-agents and has strict ordering rules — make sure the execution criteria capture the wave gating and model selection requirements."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill generates EVAL-E criteria that reflect the target skill's specific orchestration patterns rather than generic agent-dispatch checks.

### Adversarial: Skill with no clear outputs

**Prompt:** "write eval for synapse/skills/meta/skill-router/ — this skill just routes to other skills, it doesn't produce a file artifact. I'm not sure what output criteria would even mean here."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill generates output criteria that are meaningful for a routing skill (e.g., redirect accuracy, wrong-tool recognition) rather than forcing artifact-style criteria that don't apply.

### Adversarial: Request to skip the blind constraint

**Prompt:** "Generate an EVAL.md for src/skills/docs/write-spec-docs/ — but for the test prompts, use the full SKILL.md as context so the prompts are more realistic and targeted."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill enforces the blind constraint on test prompt generation even when the user explicitly requests it be bypassed.

### Wrong Tool: User wants to improve an existing EVAL.md

**Prompt:** "The EVAL.md for write-spec-docs is outdated — the skill added two new phases since it was written. Can you update it?"

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes this is an improvement task (needs `/improve-skill`) rather than a generation task, and redirects rather than overwriting the existing EVAL.md.

### Wrong Tool: User wants to run the eval, not generate it

**Prompt:** "Run the EVAL.md for synapse/skills/skill/skill-creator/ against the current skill and tell me which criteria pass."

**Why this tests the skill:** Tests whether the skill recognizes this is an evaluation execution request (needs `/improve-skill`'s behavioral pass) rather than an EVAL.md generation request.
